Off Calendar Jul-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mihir Desai of POND NORTH LLP (213-617-6170), appearing through CourtCall for defendant and moving party Genuine Parts Company.
Roger E. Gold of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1339, X-134), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiffs and opposing parties Gloria Luz Martinez, Individually and as Successor-in-Interest to Juan Sanchez Martinez, Sr., Decedent; Juan Martinez, Jr., Martin Martinez, and Francisco Martinez.

RULING: DEFENDANT GENUINE PARTS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Genuine Parts Company's Motion for Summary Adjudication as to punitive damages is GRANTED. Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855. Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses restate their allegations and simply provide laundry lists of people and/or documents without specific facts supported by evidence. (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 96, 107.) Plaintiffs fail to create a triable issue of material fact demonstrating that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Basich v. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001) 87 Cal. App. 4th 1112, 1120 [the higher evidentiary standard of clear and convincing evidence applies].) Defendant's objection to GPC's Responses to General Order 129 Standard Interrogatories is OVERRULED. (Gold Declaration, Exhibit 1.) Defendant's objection to Gloria Martinez's supplemental Responses to GPC's Special Interrogatories is SUSTAINED as the responses are unverified. (Gold Declaration, Exhibit 2.) Defendant's objection to the deposition of Paul Lecour is SUSTAINED based on relevance. (Gold Declaration, Exhibit 3.) Defendant's objection to the deposition of Byron H. Frantz is SUSTAINED based on hearsay and relevance. (Gold Declaration, Exhibit 4.) Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Jennifer Roux of US Legal Support, CSR #11033, jroux@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 415-362-4346) (503/CML)
